rulings.Case Title: LINDA S CATRON VS. NATIONSTAR MORTGAGE LLC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Extend Time 583.60(B); Cal Civ Proc 583.340
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 5, 2025. Line 1. PLAINTIFF LINDA CATRON AN INDIVIDUAL Notice Of Motion And Motion To Extend Time 583.60(B); Cal Civ Proc 583.340; is OFF CALENDAR. No proof of service on file. =(501/AAT) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: HOMERISESF VS. SYLVESTER J. HARRIS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 5, 2025. Line 13. DEFENDANT SYLVESTER HARRIS MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS; is DENIED. The notice of motion identifies CCP 430.10(b) as the statutory basis for this motion. CCP 430.10 does not apply to a motion for judgment on the pleadings. CCP 438 provides the limited permissible grounds for a motion for judgment on the pleadings. There is no parallel provision to CCP 430.10(b) in CCP 438. =(501/AAT) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: VF-1 NA ALTERNATIVE HOLDINGS LLC VS. MODESTO CERVERA ROBLES ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 5, 2025. Line 15. DEFENDANT MODESTO ROBLES DEMURRER to COMPLAINT; is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer from notice of entry of order. The Complaint states a cause of action for unlawful detainer and is not uncertain. This action is based on CCP 1161a, and nothing on the face of the Complaint alleges the existence of any tenancy or lease agreement. The demurrer does not specify any grounds for objection other than CCP 430.10(e) and 430.10(f). =(501/AAT) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
